name: django-modern-rpc

metadata.name: django-modern-rpc
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Simple and powerful RPC server for your Django project

documentation:
# django-modern-rpc

[![Downloads](https://static.pepy.tech/personalized-badge/django-modern-rpc?period=total&units=international_system&left_color=grey&right_color=orange&left_text=Downloads)](https://pepy.tech/project/django-modern-rpc)
[![Tests](https://github.com/alorence/django-modern-rpc/actions/workflows/default.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/alorence/django-modern-rpc/actions/workflows/default.yml)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/django-modern-rpc/badge/?version=latest)](https://django-modern-rpc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=main)
[![Link to demo](https://img.shields.io/badge/demo-online-informational.svg)](https://modernrpc.onrender.com)

Expose global python functions through XML-RPC and/or JSON-RPC server using Django toolbox.

## Main features

- XML-RPC and JSON-RPC 2.0 support (JSON-RPC 1.0 is NOT supported)
- HTTP Basic Auth & custom authentication methods
- Multiple entry-points: group your RPC methods under different paths to apply
  specific rules, authentication, protocol support, etc.
- API docs generation (based on docstring)

## Requirements

The following Django / Python version are supported, according to Django requirements (see
[here](https://docs.djangoproject.com/fr/3.2/faq/install/#faq-python-version-support) and
[here](https://docs.djangoproject.com/fr/4.2/faq/install/#faq-python-version-support))

| 🠗 Django \ Python 🠖   | 3.7 | 3.8 | 3.9 | 3.10 | 3.11| 3.12 | 3.13 |
|-----------------------|------|-----|-----|------|------|-----|-----|
| 2.2                   |  ✔️️  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ❌  |  ❌  |  ❌ |  ❌ |
| 3.0                   |  ✔️️  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ❌  |  ❌  |  ❌ |  ❌ |
| 3.1                   |  ✔️️  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ❌  |  ❌  |  ❌ |  ❌ |
| 3.2                   |  ✔️️  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ❌  |  ❌ |  ❌ |
| 4.0                   |  ❌  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ❌  |  ❌ |  ❌ |
| 4.1                   |  ❌  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ❌ |  ❌ |
| 4.2                   |  ❌  | ✔️️  | ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️ |  ❌ |
| 5.0                   |  ❌  | ❌  | ❌  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️ |  ✔️️ |
| 5.1                   |  ❌  | ❌  | ❌  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️  |  ✔️️ |  ✔️️ |

## Setup

A [quick start](https://django-modern-rpc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/basics/quickstart.html) is available as part
of the documentation to help setting up you project.

## Code quality

Continuous integration and code analysis is performed automatically to ensure a decent code quality. Project health
is publicly available on following apps:

[![Codacy Badge](https://app.codacy.com/project/badge/Grade/37607e2ecaf549b890fc6defca88c7f8)](https://app.codacy.com/gh/alorence/django-modern-rpc/dashboard?utm_source=gh&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=&utm_campaign=Badge_grade)
[![Codacy Badge](https://app.codacy.com/project/badge/Coverage/37607e2ecaf549b890fc6defca88c7f8)](https://app.codacy.com/gh/alorence/django-modern-rpc/dashboard?utm_source=gh&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=&utm_campaign=Badge_coverage)
[![Coveralls Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/alorence/django-modern-rpc/badge.svg)](https://coveralls.io/github/alorence/django-modern-rpc)

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.xmlhandler.XMLRPCHandler

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.xmlhandler.XMLRPCHandler.process_request
documentation:
Parse request and delegates to process_single_request(), catching exceptions to handle errors.

`system.multicall()` is implemented in `modernrpc.system_methods` module.

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.xmlhandler.XMLRPCHandler.parse_request
documentation: Parse request body to extract `(params, methodName)` tuple, returned as `RequestData`

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.xmlhandler.XMLRPCHandler.process_single_request
documentation: Check and call the RPC method, based on given tuple `(params, methodName)`

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.xmlhandler.XMLRPCHandler.dumps_result
documentation: Dumps result instance into a proper XML-RPC xml response
Default XML-RPC handler implementation

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JsonRpcDataMixin
documentation: Wraps JSON-RPC specific information used to handle requests

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JsonSuccessResult
documentation: A JSON-RPC success result

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JsonErrorResult
documentation: A JSON-RPC error result. Allows setting additional data, as specified in standard

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JSONRPCHandler

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JSONRPCHandler.process_request
documentation:
Parse request and process it, according to its kind. Standard request as well as batch request is supported.

Delegates to `process_single_request()` to perform most of the checks and procedure execution. Depending on the
result of `parse_request()`, standard or batch request will be handled here.

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JSONRPCHandler.parse_request
documentation:
Parse request body and return deserialized payload, or raise an RPCParseError

Returned payload may be a dict (for standard request) or a list of dicts (for batch request).

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JSONRPCHandler.check_request
documentation: Perform request validation raises RPCInvalidRequest on any error

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JSONRPCHandler.process_single_request
documentation: Check and call the RPC method, based on given request dict.

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.jsonhandler.JSONRPCHandler.dumps_result
documentation:
Dumps result instance into a proper JSON-RPC response

Notifications are supported here, based on result's `is_notification` member: return an empty string
Default JSON-RPC handler implementation

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.BaseResult

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.BaseResult.serializable_data
documentation:
Dumps response content into the right format. Protocol specific
implementations of this method must be written.

Base class for results returned from procedure execution

Subclass must be created for Error / Success result semantic, as well as for protocol-specific features

classes.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.get_method_wrapper
documentation:
Return RPCMethod instance corresponding to given name, from the registry

An exception is raised on any error

:raise: RPCUnknownMethod

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.valid_content_types
documentation: Return the list of content-types supported by the concrete handler

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.response_content_type
documentation: Return the Content-Type value to set in responses

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.can_handle
documentation:
Return True if this instance can handle the given request.

Default implementation will check Content-Type for supported value

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.process_request
documentation:
Fully process a request. Return the str content ready to be sent as HttpResponse.

This is the only method the view is supposed to call after choosing the right handler.
Concrete implementation must perform all work here, according to its protocol specifications.

Important: This method should delegate concrete procedure execution to process_single_request(). This will
help to implement multi-request (JSON-RPC batch / XML-RPC system.multicall) features.

Implementations of this method must ensure no exception is raised from here. All code must be secured
to return a proper RPC error response on any exception.

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.parse_request
documentation:
Parse the body of incoming request and extract data needed for execution.

The return-type of this method depends on concrete implementation.

Must raises RPCException or subclasses on errors

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.process_single_request
documentation:
Perform all mandatory task before executing a single procedure.

Check request format, retrieve RPCMethod wrapper, call the corresponding function and return the resulting data.
All errors must be caught internally, concrete implementations must not raise exception from this method.

```
param request_data

Request information, as returned by parse_request() method

param context

Information needed to execution procedure

return

A result instance (Error or Success)
```

methods.name: modernrpc.handlers.base.RPCHandler.dumps_result
documentation:
Serialize the result instance (Error or Success) into a string ready to be returned to HTTP response.

All errors must be caught internally and converted to proper error response. Concrete implementations must
not raise exception from this method.
Base class for concrete RPC Handlers. Provide an interface as well as some common methods implementations.

functions.name: modernrpc.helpers.get_builtin_date
documentation:
Try to convert a date to a builtin instance of `datetime.datetime`.
The input date can be a `str`, a `datetime.datetime`, a `xmlrpc.client.Datetime` or a `xmlrpclib.Datetime`
instance. The returned object is a `datetime.datetime`.

```
param date

The date object to convert.

param date_format

If the given date is a str, format is passed to strptime to parse it

param raise_exception

If set to True, an exception will be raised if the input string cannot be parsed

return

A valid datetime.datetime instance
```

functions.name: modernrpc.helpers.ensure_sequence
documentation: Ensure the given argument is a sequence object (tuple, list). If not, return a list containing its value.

functions.name: modernrpc.helpers.first_true
documentation:
Same as more_itertools.first_true(), but avoid dependency to a new lib
Doc: https://more-itertools.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#more_itertools.first_true

modules.name: modernrpc.exceptions
documentation:
Error handling is fully described in both XML & JSON-RPC standards. Each common error have an associated *faultCode*
and the response format is described, so errors can be handled correctly on the client side.

In django-modern-rpc, all errors are reported using a set of pre-defined exceptions. Thus, in JSON and XML-RPC handlers,
when an exception is caught, the correct error response is returned to the view and transmitted to the client.

This simplifies error management, and allow developers to simply return errors to clients from inside a RPC Method.
The error codes values are defined in:

- <http://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#error_object> for JSON-RPC
- <http://xmlrpc-epi.sourceforge.net/specs/rfc.fault_codes.php> for XML-RPC

Pre-defined exceptions uses the following error codes:

```
RPC_PARSE_ERROR = -32700
RPC_INVALID_REQUEST = -32600
RPC_METHOD_NOT_FOUND = -32601
RPC_INVALID_PARAMS = -32602
RPC_INTERNAL_ERROR = -32603

# Used as minimal value for any custom error returned by the server
RPC_CUSTOM_ERROR_BASE = -32099
# Used as maximal value for any custom error returned by the server
RPC_CUSTOM_ERROR_MAX = -32000
```
JSON-RPC error codes
From https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification#error_object
-32700              Parse error 	    Invalid JSON was received by the server.
An error occurred on the server while parsing the JSON text.
-32600              Invalid Request 	The JSON sent is not a valid Request object.
-32601              Method not found 	The method does not exist / is not available.
-32602              Invalid params 	    Invalid method parameter(s).
-32603              Internal error 	    Internal JSON-RPC error.
-32000 to -32099 	Server error 	    Reserved for implementation-defined server-errors.
XML-RPC error codes
From http://xmlrpc-epi.sourceforge.net/specs/rfc.fault_codes.php
-32700 ---> parse error. not well formed
-32701 ---> parse error. unsupported encoding
-32702 ---> parse error. invalid character for encoding
-32600 ---> server error. invalid xml-rpc. not conforming to spec.
-32601 ---> server error. requested method not found
-32602 ---> server error. invalid method parameters
-32603 ---> server error. internal xml-rpc error
-32500 ---> application error
-32400 ---> system error
-32300 ---> transport error
In addition, the range -32099 .. -32000, inclusive is reserved for implementation defined server errors. Server
errors which do not cleanly map to a specific error defined by this spec should be assigned to a number in this range.
This leaves the remainder of the space available for application defined errors.

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.RPCException
documentation:
This is the base class of all RPC exception. Custom exceptions raised by your RPC methods
should inherits from RPCException.

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.RPCParseError
documentation: Raised by handlers if the request can't be read as valid JSON or XML data.

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.RPCInvalidRequest
documentation: Raised by handlers if incoming JSON or XML data is not a valid JSON-RPC or XML-RPC data.

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.RPCMethodNotFound
documentation: Raised by handlers the RPC method called is not defined for the current entry point and protocol.

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.RPCInvalidParams
documentation: Raised by handlers if the RPC method's params does not match the parameters in RPC request

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.RPCInternalError
documentation: Raised by handlers if any standard exception is raised during the execution of the RPC method.

classes.name: modernrpc.exceptions.AuthenticationFailed
documentation: Raised when authentication system forbade execution of a RPC Method

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.set_authentication_predicate
documentation:
Assign a new authentication predicate to an RPC method.
This is the most generic decorator used to implement authentication.
Predicate is a standard function with the following signature:

```python
def my_predicate(request, *params):
    # Inspect request and extract required information

    if <condition>:
        # The condition to execute the method are met
        return True
    return False
```

```
param predicate



param params



return

```
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.user_has_all_perms
documentation: Returns True if the given user have all given permissions

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.user_in_group
documentation: Returns True if the given user is in given group

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.user_in_any_group
documentation: Returns True if the given user is in at least 1 of the given groups

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.user_in_all_groups
documentation: Returns True if the given user is in all given groups

modules.name: modernrpc.introspection
documentation:
Handle all introspection related features on rpc_methods:
- Help text
- Signature

classes.name: modernrpc.introspection.Introspector

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.Introspector.accept_kwargs
documentation: Determine if function signature contains **kwargs special argument

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.Introspector.args
documentation: List all function arguments

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.Introspector.return_type
documentation: Return the return type as defined from signature typehint
Helper to extract signature and type hint for given function

classes.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.raw_docstring
documentation: Return full content of function docstring, as extracted by inspect.getdoc()

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.cleanup_spaces
documentation: Basically, replaces any number of consecutive space chars (space, newline, tab, etc.) to a single space

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.text_documentation
documentation: Return the text part of the docstring block, excluding legacy typehints and parameters and return docs

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.html_documentation
documentation: Convert the text part of the docstring to an HTML representation, using the parser set in settings

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.args_doc
documentation:
Return a dict with argument name as key and documentation text as value. The dict will contain only
documented arguments.
Basically this method parse and extract reST documented arguments:

> ```
> param <argname>
>
> Documentation for <argname>
> ```

Alternatively, it also supports @param format:

> @param \<argname>: Documentation for \<argname>

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.args_types
documentation:
Return a dict with argument name as key and documented type as value. The dict will contain only
documented arguments.
Basically this method parse and extract reST doctype documentation:

> ```
> type <argname>
>
> int or str
> ```

Alternatively, it also supports @type format:

> @type \<argname>: int or str

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.return_doc
documentation:
Return the documentation for method return value, as found in docstring:

> ```
> return
>
> Documentation on return value
> ```

Alternatively, it also supports @return format:

> @return: Documentation on return value

Return an empty string if return documentation is not found

methods.name: modernrpc.introspection.DocstringParser.return_type
documentation:
Return the documentation for method return type, as found in docstring:

> ```
> rtype
>
> list
> ```

Alternatively, it also supports @rtype format:

> @rtype: list

Return an empty string if return type information is not found

Parse docstring to extract params docs & types and return doc & type. It also converts
long docstring part to an HTML representation using parser from settings (markdown/rst)

classes.name: modernrpc.views.RPCEntryPoint

methods.name: modernrpc.views.RPCEntryPoint.handler_classes
documentation: Return the list of handlers to use when receiving RPC requests.

methods.name: modernrpc.views.RPCEntryPoint.post
documentation:
Handle an XML-RPC or JSON-RPC request.

```
param request

Incoming request

param args

Unused

param kwargs

Unused

return

A HttpResponse containing XML-RPC or JSON-RPC response with the result of procedure call
```

methods.name: modernrpc.views.RPCEntryPoint.get_context_data
documentation:
Update context data with list of RPC methods of the current entry point.
Will be used to display methods documentation page
This is the main entry point class. It inherits standard Django View class.
Disables CSRF validation for POST requests

functions.name: modernrpc.apps.check_settings
documentation:
Perform common checks on lib configuration, to notify for warning and errors. Uses
Django "System check framework": see https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.2/topics/checks/

methods.name: modernrpc.apps.ModernRpcConfig.rpc_methods_registration
documentation: Store all decorated RPC methods as well as builtin system methods into internal registry

methods.name: modernrpc.apps.ModernRpcConfig.import_modules
documentation:
Loop on given modules_list, import each module and register
functions annotated with @rpc_method in the internal registry

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_check_user
documentation:
Inspect the given request and extract the user / password from HTTP_AUTHORIZATION header.
Then try to use it to authenticate against Django User model. If user can be authenticated, it is returned.
If no user is found, an anonymous user object is returned.
\:param request: The request to inspect
\:param user_validation_func:
\:return:

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_get_user
documentation:
Inspect the given request to find a logged user. If not found, the header HTTP_AUTHORIZATION
is read for 'Basic Auth' login and password, and try to authenticate against default UserModel.
Always return a User instance (possibly anonymous, meaning authentication failed)

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_login_required
documentation: Decorator. Use it to specify a RPC method is available only to logged users
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_superuser_required
documentation: Decorator. Use it to specify a RPC method is available only to logged superusers
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_permissions_required
documentation: Decorator. Use it to specify a RPC method is available only to logged users with given permissions
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_any_of_permissions_required
documentation: Decorator. Use it to specify a RPC method is available only to logged users with given permissions
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_group_member_required
documentation: Decorator. Use it to specify a RPC method is available only to logged users with given permissions
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.auth.basic.http_basic_auth_all_groups_member_required
documentation: Decorator. Use it to specify a RPC method is available only to logged users with given permissions
Decorator

functions.name: modernrpc.system_methods.__system_list_methods
documentation: Returns a list of all methods available in the current entry point

functions.name: modernrpc.system_methods.__system_method_signature
documentation:
Returns an array describing the signature of the given method name.

The result is an array with:
: - Return type as first elements
  - Types of method arguments from element 1 to N

```
param method_name

Name of a method available for current entry point (and protocol)

param kwargs



return

An array of arrays describing types of return values and method arguments
```

functions.name: modernrpc.system_methods.__system_method_help
documentation:
Returns the documentation of the given method name.

```
param method_name

Name of a method available for current entry point (and protocol)

param kwargs



return

Documentation text for the RPC method
```

functions.name: modernrpc.system_methods.__system_multi_call
documentation:
Call multiple RPC methods at once.

```
param calls

An array of struct like {"methodName": string, "params": array }

param kwargs

Internal data

return

```

functions.name: modernrpc.core.rpc_method
documentation:
Mark a standard python function as RPC method.

All arguments are optional

```
param func

A standard function

param name

Used as RPC method name instead of original function name

param entry_point

Default: ALL. Used to limit usage of the RPC method for a specific set of entry points

param protocol

Default: ALL. Used to limit usage of the RPC method for a specific protocol (JSONRPC or XMLRPC)
```

functions.name: modernrpc.core.register_rpc_method
documentation: For backward compatibility. Use registry.register_method() instead (with same arguments)
Backward compatibility.
In release 0.11.0, following global functions have been moved to a proper _RPCRegistry class,
instantiated as a global "registry". For backward compatibility

functions.name: modernrpc.core.get_all_method_names
documentation: For backward compatibility. Use registry.get_all_method_names() instead (with same arguments)

functions.name: modernrpc.core.get_all_methods
documentation: For backward compatibility. Use registry.get_all_methods() instead (with same arguments)

functions.name: modernrpc.core.get_method
documentation: For backward compatibility. Use registry.get_method() instead (with same arguments)

functions.name: modernrpc.core.reset_registry
documentation: For backward compatibility. Use registry.reset() instead

classes.name: modernrpc.core.Protocol
documentation: Define a custom type to use everywhere a protocol (JSON-RPC or XML-RPC) is expected

classes.name: modernrpc.core.RPCRequestContext
documentation:
Wraps all information needed to call a procedure. Instances of this class are created before call,
and may be used to populate kwargs dict in rpc method.

classes.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.check_permissions
documentation:
Call the predicate(s) associated with the RPC method, to check if the current request
can actually call the method.
Return a boolean indicating if the method should be executed (True) or not (False)

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.available_for_protocol
documentation: Check if the current function can be executed from a request through the given protocol

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.args
documentation: Method arguments

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.raw_docstring
documentation: Method docstring, as raw text

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.html_doc
documentation: Methods docstring, as HTML

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.args_doc
documentation:
Build an OrderedDict mapping each method argument with its
corresponding type (from typehint or doctype) and documentation.

methods.name: modernrpc.core.RPCMethod.return_doc
documentation: Build a dict for method's return type and documentation
Wraps a python global function to be used to extract information and call the concrete procedure.

methods.name: modernrpc.core._RPCRegistry.register_method
documentation:
Register a function to be available as RPC method.

The given function will be inspected to find external_name, protocol and entry_point values set by the decorator
@rpc_method.
\:param func: A function previously decorated using @rpc_method
\:return: The name of registered method

methods.name: modernrpc.core._RPCRegistry.get_all_method_names
documentation: Return the names of all RPC methods registered supported by the given entry_point / protocol pair

methods.name: modernrpc.core._RPCRegistry.get_all_methods
documentation: Return a list of all methods in the registry supported by the given entry_point / protocol pair

methods.name: modernrpc.core._RPCRegistry.get_method
documentation: Retrieve a method from the given name
